  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Troweling bleed water back into the concrete surface, which will cause dusting, scaling, and a severely weakened surface layer."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop finishing operations immediately.",
    "Allow bleed water to evaporate naturally or remove it using a hose, squeegee, or dragging a burlap/rope across the surface.",
    "Do not resume troweling until all bleed water has disappeared and the concrete has set sufficiently."
  ],
  "rationale": "Finishing concrete while bleed water is still present on the surface traps water under the trowel, increasing the water-cement ratio at the surface. This leads to low strength, dusting, scaling, and delamination, violating standard concrete finishing practices.",
  "references": [
    "ACI 302.1R",
    "ACI 308R"
  ]
}
